criteria.label: Explain split-apply-combine and when to use agg vs transform.
criteria.example: In one paragraph, contrast `groupby('store')['sales'].mean()` (agg) vs `.transform('mean')` and state which preserves row count.

criteria.label: Perform named aggregations and merge summaries onto a base table.
criteria.example: From orders with customer_id and amount, produce total_spend per customer with as_index=False and left-merge onto a customers table.

criteria.label: Choose and justify inner vs left merge for a given fact/dimension scenario.
criteria.example: Given events + products, write `pd.merge(events, products, on='product_id', how='left')` and explain why inner would drop unmatched events.

criteria.label: Detect and fix a many-to-many merge row explosion.
criteria.example: Show `len(merged) > len(left)` after merge, identify duplicate keys on the right with `meta['user_id'].duplicated().sum()`, and fix keys or dedupe before re-merge.

criteria.label: Refactor a leaky global groupby feature into a split-first, train-only pipeline.
criteria.example: Replace full-data `transform('mean')` with train_test_split, then `train.groupby('user_id')['x'].mean().map` onto train and test columns separately.
